behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module state handoff (git-diff/age computation + PR-state check + staleness rule). This is not a single pure function, so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network is not required by the behavior text itself, so System is not forced (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a/b: no explicit real infra or model output requirement). Rejection gate for System: behavior text does not require "live" repo/API; it states rule correctness, which can be caught with wired modules and controlled fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire branch-state calculator, age-threshold config, and PR-state provider together with fixture branch metadata and PR states; assert stale/no-stale outcomes for combinations of (ahead-of-main, age, PR-open).

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is bootstrap orchestration: session-start hook + stale-state service + renderer/emitter. Failure appears at module wiring/propagation boundary (MOCK-MISS), not in one local function. No explicit real subprocess/network dependency in wording, so System is not required. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start setup in-process with a stale-state fixture and assert emitted warning contains branch name, age/context, and actionable next step text.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets one local deterministic mapping function. No module handoff, I/O, subprocess, network, or model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over branch-state inputs asserting exact label output (`clean` vs `stale-no-pr`) and determinism.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo and live PR state. Real git/OS/subprocess and real external API interaction are on the failure boundary (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE), so Integration is too low. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end against an actual test repo plus real PR backend state; verify rendered output classification and stale marker text for clean vs unPRed-stale branches.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is rule integration between branch-state evaluator and PR-status interpretation (open/merged exclusion). This is cross-module logic, so Unit is too low. System was considered, but behavior text does not require live backend; open/merged states can be represented via controlled provider fixtures (rejection gate for System). No AI/multi-agent dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with wired staleness evaluator + PR-status adapter using fixture states (`open`, `merged`, `none`) and assert only `none` can be stale-no-pr.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary depends on real VCS history transformations (rebase/squash merge hash rewrites). These are OS/filesystem/subprocess git behaviors that mocks often hide (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE), so Integration is typically insufficient. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create real repo history, perform actual rebase/squash-merge flows, then run staleness detection and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale after integration into main-equivalent history.
